memoryArtifacts.headline: MADDOX/MADDUX Nabb Research Center Article PT2

memoryArtifacts.description:
Warwicks Warrosquyoake Squrake Squeak
16 FEB 1623

Warwick Squrake, Jamestown, Virginia

Page 58 Colonial records of Virginia  by Virginia. General Assembly. Joint Committee on the State Library; Virginia. General Assembly. Senate; Wynne, Thomas Hicks, d. 1875  

A LIST OF NAMES OF THE DEAD IN VIRGINIA SINCE APRIL LAST 
16 FEB 1723
[Maybe Where His DEATH DATE Was Created From]

https://archive.org/stream/colonialrecordso00virg#page/58/mode/2up/search/melfon

"Warwick Squrake Plantation" Warrosquoake Shire (with numerous variant spellings, including Warrascoyack, Warrascocke and "Warwick Squeak") One of Virginia's Eight Original Shires Later, It was renamed Isle of Wight County in 1637. The eight original shires were: Accawmack, Charles City, Charles River, Elizabeth City, Henrico, James City, Warrosquyoake, and Warwick River.

 Edward Bennett, an English merchant and a free member of the London Company, was among those who got a land patent and founded his plantation in 1621.[1] He named his plantation Warrosquoake,[1] after the river which the indigenous people called by that name. His plantation suffered high fatalities of colonists in the Great Massacre of 1622, losing 53 persons. A total of the 347 colonists were killed that day, as the Powhatan tribes tried to kill and expel the English.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Maddox於1590年6月28日出生在英國倫敦時，他的父親John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach18歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Aubrey20歲。他於1611年與Lady Rebecca Notley在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1619年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州。他去世於1623年2月16日之前在美國維吉尼亞州詹姆斯 希迪加斯托，埋葬在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾利費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Maddox
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): from the Welsh personal name Madog (see Maddock ). Variant of Maddocks .

variants.name: Mattix
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

variants.name: Maddock
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): from the Welsh personal name Madawc, Madog, from the ancestor of Welsh madog ‘goodly’. As a personal name and surname it was quite common in medieval Wales, in English counties bordering Wales, and in late medieval West Cornwall.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: On December 6, 1606, the journey to Virginia began on three ships: the Susan Constant, the Godspeed, and the Discovery. In 1607, 104 English men and boys arrived in North America to start a settlement. On May 13 they picked Jamestown, Virginia for their settlement, which was named after their King, James I. The settlement became the first permanent English settlement in North America... In 1698, fire struck Jamestown. The fire was evidently started by a prisoner awaiting execution in the nearby prison. The fire destroyed the prison and the statehouse, though many of the public records were saved. In 1699, the government and capital were moved from Jamestown to Middle Plantation, renamed Williamsburg. People continued to live on Jamestown Island and owned farm lands, but it ceased to be a town.
historicEvents.urlTitle: National Park Service - Historic Jamestowne - A Short History of Jamestown

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Maddox was born on 28 June 1590, in London, England, his father, John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach, was 18 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Aubrey, was 20. He married Lady Rebecca Notley in 1611, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 3 daughters. He immigrated to Virginia, British Colonial America in 1619. He died before 16 February 1623, in Jamestown, James City, Virginia, United States, and was buried in Lichfield, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Maddox est né le 28 juin 1590, à Londres, Angleterre, son père, John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Aubrey, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Lady Rebecca Notley en 1611, à Londres, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 3 filles. Il a immigré au Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1619. Il est décédé avant 16 février 1623, à Jamestown, James City, Virginie, États-Unis, et a été enterré à Lichfield, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Maddoxが1590年6月28日にイギリスロンドンで生まれた時，父John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynachは18歳，母Lady Elizabeth Aubreyは20歳であった。1611年にイギリスロンドンでLady Rebecca Notleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1619年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州に移住した。彼は1623年2月16日以前にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州ジャムス シトイジャムストウンで死去し，イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアリチフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Maddox(이)가 1590년 6월 28일 영국 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Aubrey(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1611년 영국 런던에서 Lady Rebecca Notley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1619년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주로 이민을 떠났다. 그는 1623년 2월 16일 이전 미국 버지니아 주 자메스 시티 자메스타운에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 리ch필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Maddox nasceu em 28 de junho de 1590, em London, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Aubrey, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Rebecca Notley em 1611, em London, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele imigrou para o Virginia, British Colonial America em 1619. Ele faleceu antes 16 de fevereiro de 1623, em Jamestown, James City, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América, e foi sepultado em Lichfield, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.London, London, England: лондон, лондон тоуншип, монро, Мичиган, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Maddox родился 28 июня 1590 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, его отцу по имени John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Aubrey, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Rebecca Notley в 1611 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он иммигрировал в Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1619 году. Он умер перед 16 февраль 1623 г. в местности джеместоун, джемесь сити, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты и был похоронен в местности личфиелд, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Jamestown, James City, Virginia, United: Jamestown, James City, Virginia, Estados Unidos
es.London, London, England: London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Estados Unidos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Maddox nació el 28 de junio de 1590, en London, Inglaterra, su papá, John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Aubrey, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Lady Rebecca Notley en 1611, en London, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 3 hijas. Inmigró a Virginia, British Colonial America en 1619. Murió antes de 16 de febrero de 1623, en Jamestown, James City, Virginia, Estados Unidos, y fue sepultado en Lichfield, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
